mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels keep the equity discount rate compressed, sustaining risk-asset valuations. As long as the funds rate remains below 0.25% and shows no upward drift over the trailing month, forward 5-day S&P 500 returns skew positive because financing conditions remain accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.12 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A first-time crossing of FEDFUNDS above 0.25%, ending the multi-year zero-rate regime, signals the onset of a monetary tightening cycle. Investors reprice discount rates upward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days as the market digests the regime shift.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% for the first time since the 2008 zero-rate policy period (liftoff event)
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12 with market anticipating December 2015 liftoff
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the liftoff crossing are positive in more than 55% of historical analogous liftoff episodes, or Rank IC is not distinguishable from zero (|IC|<0.03) in the 6 months surrounding the event.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below the 5.0% threshold associated with full employment, wage-cost pressure expectations rise, prompting anticipation of faster Fed tightening. This dampens equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% for at least two consecutive monthly readings
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=5.0 as of t-1mo, at the edge of full-employment threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after UNRATE first breaches below 5.0% are positive on average across historical cycles, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS month-over-month increase) occurring simultaneously with a declining UNRATE jointly signal a tightening labor market that raises the probability of imminent Fed rate hikes beyond what either series indicates alone. This joint signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as rate-hike odds are repriced.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS m/m change > 200k AND UNRATE declines or holds at multi-year low in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=142654k with UNRATE=5.0 signaling tight labor market ahead of expected liftoff
regime_hint: labor market tightening ahead of monetary liftoff
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-strength/UNRATE-decline signal fails to produce below-median 20-day returns in a majority of historical occurrences, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from the sum of individual series' IC (delta IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects robust consumer spending momentum, a leading indicator of corporate revenue and earnings strength. This persistent uptrend positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=447255 million as of t-1mo showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing RSAFS growth rate and 60-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns following RSAFS uptrends are not significantly higher than during downtrends (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST housing starts signal expanding construction activity and household formation, a positive demand-side leading indicator for equities specifically during early-cycle expansions when credit is accessible and rates are low. In late-cycle regimes with rising rates, the same signal loses predictive power or reverses because housing becomes rate-sensitive and vulnerable to tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1206k as of t-2mo with FEDFUNDS still near zero, consistent with early-to-mid cycle conditions
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The positive HOUST-return relationship fails to hold (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the sign of the relationship flips to negative in more than one of the last three low-rate expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in INDPRO (industrial production index) reflects expanding supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing output, signaling broad economic growth that supports corporate earnings and positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO trend and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or forward returns during INDPRO expansions are statistically indistinguishable from returns during contractions.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between a near-zero FEDFUNDS rate and subdued CPIAUCSL inflation growth implies a deeply negative or near-zero real policy rate, which supports risk-asset valuations. If CPIAUCSL growth accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains anchored near zero, real rates fall further, providing an additional short-term tailwind for S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days; if inflation instead decelerates alongside the low rate, the easing tailwind weakens.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.12 and CPIAUCSL=237.566 as of t-2mo, implying near-zero or negative real policy rate
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary policy with low inflation
failure_condition: The implied real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL y/y change) shows no significant relationship with 5-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC<0.02), or the interaction effect's marginal IC contribution over the individual series is below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.
